general_facts: App uses Firebase Realtime Database with dual-path normalization (essentials vs history).
Essentials: Static data and foreign keys at /entity/$id.
History: Transactional logs and frequent changes at /entity_history/$id.
Loading indicators are managed via view.emit('loading:start') and view.emit('loading:end') in main.js.
Global loader element is #global-loader in index.html.
Product Types are stored in the normalized RTDB structure.
Frontend controller needs to support emit() for event handling.
All views MUST emit view.emit('rendered') after data loads. This triggers applyAuthorities() in main.js via view.on('rendered', applyAuthorities).
NEVER use document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('apply-auth')) in views — always use view.emit('rendered') instead.
Both view.triggers.js and view.forms.js require import { createModal } from '../lib/modal.js' — they use it for delete confirm dialogs.

tech_stack.frontend: React-like controller library (custom), Bootstrap 5, D3.js (Dashboard), Leaflet.js (Vans/Appointments)

Events.Q: Why is view.emit not a function?
Events.A: The custom controller library missing event emitter capability.

Events.Q: How does auth get re-applied after a view loads?
Events.A: View emits 'rendered' after data arrives. main.js listens with view.on('rendered', applyAuthorities). Never use document.dispatchEvent directly.
